qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unknown-group recursion in decode() exhausts the JavaScript call stack
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is a byte-decoding availability bug in `src/decoder.js` and `src/reader.js` where unknown group fields recurse through `Reader.skipType(3)` without a depth limit. The advisory instead concerns malicious JSON descriptors reaching runtime code generation through reflection APIs and the upstream fix sanitizes `Type` names in `src/type.js` before generated functions are built.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: pbts executes attacker-controlled filenames through child_process.exec
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is a CLI shell-injection bug in `cli/pbts.js` where attacker-controlled filenames are concatenated into a command string and passed to `child_process.exec`. The advisory root cause is in protobufjs reflection and descriptor loading (`Root.fromJSON` to `Type` runtime code generation), not the `pbts` CLI wrapper.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No finalized qualifying finding identifies the advisory's vulnerable reflection/code-generation path. GHSA-xq3m-2v4x-88gg is about crafted JSON descriptors loaded through reflection APIs such as `Root.fromJSON`, with untrusted type metadata flowing into runtime code generation; the only qualifying findings here are an unknown-group decode stack exhaustion bug and a separate `pbts` CLI shell injection bug.
evidence: The advisory describes arbitrary code execution from crafted protobuf JSON descriptors loaded through reflection APIs, and upstream fix commit `535df44` adds `name = name.replace(/\\W/g, "")` in `src/type.js` before generated functions are constructed.

In the vulnerable tree, `Root.fromJSON` in `src/root.js` loads namespace descriptors with `root.addJSON(json.nested).resolveAll()`, and `Type.generateConstructor` in `src/type.js` passes `mtype.name` into `util.codegen`, which is the advisory's runtime-codegen boundary.

The `decode_unknown_group_stack_exhaustion` finding instead traces `Type.decode()` default unknown-field handling in `src/decoder.js` to recursive `Reader.skipType(3)` logic in `src/reader.js`, which is a distinct availability flaw on binary message decoding.

The `pbts_filename_command_injection` finding instead traces `cli/pbts.js` command construction to `child_process.exec`, which is a distinct CLI shell-injection sink unrelated to descriptor loading or reflection code generation.
